intro: Personal finance follows universal principles: compound interest, diversification, long investment horizons, and low fees. These principles work in any global market. This calculator estimates the annual yield for Argentine Bonos (AL30) and Global bonds (GD30). Values are current as of 2026 and reviewed regularly to keep you informed.
keyTakeaway: Formula: **Bond IRR**. Direct application with your inputs. Calculates estimated annual yields for AL30 and GD30 bonds based on purchase price, coupon, and years to maturity.

fields.label: Years to maturity
fields.help: Number of years until bond maturity.

explanation:
Personal finance and bond investing use universal mathematical principles (compound interest, time value of money, risk-return optimization). This calculator applies industry-standard bond yield formulas.

## How It's Calculated

The calculator estimates annual yields for Argentine Bonares (AL30) and Global bonds (GD30) using the internal rate of return (IRR) formula. It applies the formula with your inputs and flags values that fall outside typical ranges.

## Typical Yield Benchmarks

| Timeline | S&P 500 | 10-Year Bonds | Cash |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 year | Highly variable | 4–5% | 3–5% |
| 5 years | ~8% average | ~4% | Inflation |
| 10 years | ~9–10% | ~4% | Below inflation |
| 20+ years | ~10% nominal | ~5% | N/A |

## Core Investment Principles

- **Stick to your plan** during crises. Emotion is the worst advisor.
- **Diversify**: don't concentrate in one asset, sector, or country.
- **Keep costs low**: annual fees above 1% eat 30% of returns over 30 years.
- **Long time horizon**: stocks work over 10+ years, not one year.
- **Rebalance once yearly**: no more, no less.

## Final Notes

This calculation is a reference guide only. For critical financial, medical, or legal decisions, consult a qualified professional. Values are current as of 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.q: What's the difference between AL30 and GD30 bonds?
faq.a: AL30 is governed under Argentine law (Bonares), while GD30 is governed under New York law (Global). GD30 typically trades at a premium due to lower legal risk.

faq.q: How do I calculate bond yield to maturity?
faq.a: Bond yield (IRR) is the annual percentage return if you hold the bond until maturity. It factors in the purchase price, coupon payments, and face value at maturity.
Bond parity is the price as a percentage of face value (100). A parity of 62% means the bond trades at 62 cents on the dollar.

faq.q: What are the main risks of Argentine sovereign bonds?
faq.a: Sovereign risk: Argentina has a history of default and restructuring. Always consider this when investing in Argentine bonds.

faq.q: How often do AL30 and GD30 pay interest?
faq.a: Both bonds pay step-up coupons that increase over time. AL30 pays 0.75%, 1.75%, 4.25%, and 5% in different years. Payments are made semiannually.
Principal begins to be repaid in semiannual installments starting in 2027.

faq.q: Where can I buy Argentine bonds like AL30 and GD30?
faq.a: Through a local or international broker with a brokerage account. Popular options in Argentina include IOL, PPI, Cocos, and Balanz.

faq.q: What average returns should I expect from bonds in 2026?
faq.a: Historical S&P 500 returns: ~10% nominal annually (~7% adjusted for inflation). Past returns don't predict the future, but they provide a reference point for planning.

sources.name: NIST — National Institute of Standards and Technology

dataUpdate.notes: Fixed formula, no external data updates required
